question_text: A student compares two devices: Device X converts 1000J of input energy into 900J of useful energy, while Device Y converts 1000J of input energy into 700J of useful energy. Calculate the efficiency of both devices and explain which is better and why efficiency matters in real applications.
correct_answer: Device X: 90% efficiency. Device Y: 70% efficiency. Device X is better because it wastes less energy (100J vs 300J). Efficiency matters because it reduces operating costs and environmental impact by minimizing wasted energy.
explanation: Device X converts 90% of input to useful output (900J/1000J = 0.90 = 90%), while Device Y is only 70% efficient (700J/1000J = 0.70 = 70%). More efficient devices save money on electricity bills and reduce the need for cooling systems to remove waste heat, making them better for both economics and the environment.
